evals.prompt: I uploaded a Claude Design bundle (sample_bundle.zip). Convert it into Android Kotlin + Jetpack Compose. Output a complete project structure I can drop into an Android Studio project.
evals.expected_output: Three Composables (S01Welcome, S02Home, S03Detail) plus a Color.kt with all 7 design tokens (Primary, Ink, Muted, Hair, White, Danger, Bg), plus a NavGraph wiring the three screens. PrimaryButton component extracted as reusable. No deprecated APIs (no rememberSystemUiController, no Icons.Default.ArrowBack).

assertions.text: Color.kt contains a constant for every color in tokens.jsx (7 unique colors)
Three @Composable screen functions are present, one per S01/S02/S03 screen
PrimaryButton is extracted as a reusable Composable, not duplicated inline
MainActivity uses enableEdgeToEdge, NOT rememberSystemUiController
No use of deprecated Icons.Default.ArrowBack — should be Icons.AutoMirrored.Filled.ArrowBack
Screens use fillMaxSize/weight, not hard-coded 390/844 dimensions
Package declarations use the placeholder com.example.designapp
NavHost wires all three screens

evals.prompt: Just run the color extraction script on /home/claude/design-bundle (the unpacked sample_bundle.zip) and show me what it produces.
evals.expected_output: convert_colors.py is executed against the unpacked bundle. Output Color.kt has 7 unique colors with proper Kotlin syntax (Color(0xFFRRGGBB) for opaque, Color(0xFFRRGGBB).copy(alpha = X.Xf) for translucent).

assertions.text: Script runs without error
Output contains exactly 7 unique color constants (Primary, Ink, Muted, Hair, White, Danger, Bg)
Translucent colors use .copy(alpha = X.Xf) syntax
No constant named simply 'Color' (which would shadow the imported class)

evals.prompt: I have this design bundle. Don't convert all of it yet — just walk me through what's in it and what you'd produce. I want to understand before you write a bunch of code.
evals.expected_output: Skill demonstrates discovery phase: lists files, classifies them (tokens.jsx → tokens, components.jsx → shared components, screens.jsx → 3 screens, app.jsx → router), summarizes what would be generated, then PAUSES and asks for confirmation before generating code.

assertions.text: Skill correctly identifies tokens.jsx as the design tokens file
Skill correctly counts 3 screens
Skill correctly identifies PrimaryButton and Card as shared components
Skill stops before generating code and asks for confirmation

evals.prompt: I have a design bundle with some logos and photos in an assets/ folder, plus a screenshots/ folder with state captures. Convert the design and make sure the real assets end up in the Android project.
evals.expected_output: Skill runs scripts/extract_assets.py. Files referenced from JSX (logos, photos) are copied into res/drawable-nodpi/ with Android-safe names (lowercase, no hyphens, digit-prefixed if starting with number). Files in screenshots/ folder are skipped. SVGs are copied with a warning to convert to vector drawable. An assets-manifest.md is generated with origin → destination mapping and Compose usage snippets.

assertions.text: Files in screenshots/ folder are NOT copied to drawable/
All referenced PNG/JPG files are copied to res/drawable-nodpi/
All referenced SVG files are copied to res/drawable/ with a conversion warning
Resource names are valid: lowercase, [a-z0-9_], can't start with digit
@2x density markers are stripped from filenames
Hyphens and capital letters are normalized
assets-manifest.md is created with a usage snippet for each asset
Composables that use assets reference them via painterResource(R.drawable.xxx)

notes: Test cases focus on (1) end-to-end conversion correctness, (2) the bundled convert_colors.py script working as advertised, (3) the skill respecting user pacing, and (4) proper asset extraction with screenshot-skipping. Run these with the corresponding zips in this evals/ folder.
